This is the perfect webinar, the framework and kind of how this whole thing fits in. If you understand this, it's kind of a high level view. So I'll kind of go through the different parts. First off, there is the introduction where the goal is to build rapport, help them identify with you, get them, all these kind of things happen initially. Then we move over here to the content section, which is where we bring out the one thing. We tell our origin story, we transition secret number one, secret number two, secret number three, and this is the content. This typically takes about an hour to do. Then from there, we transition to the stack and closes, which is this part right here. So we're going to go through this. Now one thing I want to preface, sometimes when people get in and say, they forget the core lesson we talked about today or yesterday, I'm not even sure anymore, that we talked about earlier about the one thing. The presentation, the goal is not try to convince them of a thousand things, it comes back into the one thing. So Jason Flattlen taught me this. He said, the idea is to have a single point of belief that your message is built around and emphasize over and over and over again from a variety of different angles. So we're still coming back to the one thing. There's just one thing we got to convince them of, which is your big domino statement. So here's your one thing. If I can get them to believe this, then they have to give me money. So then it comes back to this and then all the other things, all the stories, everything we're doing are all the things that are making them believe this. It's all these different things that are coming back to this one thing. So don't think you're trying to convince them of this and this and this and this. As soon as you start doing the and and and and, it gets all sorts of, it makes the offer muddy, it makes it hard to convert. It always comes back down to one thing. It's having one idea, one single point of belief that we got to get them to believe and we're emphasizing it from whole bunch of different directions. And that's what, if we do it right, knock down the big domino, boom, they have belief and then they join our culture. That's how it works. Cool. Any question about that? It should make sense. All right. So there it is again. And we're going to go through. Does everybody have this on your computer? We downloaded it. So the back store behind that, I learned from Ted Thomas. If you guys Google Ted Thomas like closing, there's videos around YouTube where he'll be like a Robert Kiyosaki event and he'll have a line of people, like 200 people following him with their credit cards in hand and they're all waving it. And he's like, come on back. And he walks to the back of the room and they call him the Pied Piper of sales because everyone's doing it. So I was in an event like this and I was speaking, I was selling, I looked in the back and I saw Ted Thomas and I was like, this has got awkward. Like I'm pitching and like the dude is like right there and I did my thing and afterwards he's like, Russell, I want to take you to lunch. I'm like, okay. So we go there and he starts asking me all these questions and we're going through lunch, we're talking and he's like, what are you doing right now? I'm like, what do you mean? He's like, look at your head. And I was like, oh, it's moving around. He's like, you've been nodding since we started talking. I'm like, okay. And he's like, when I was in your room, he's like, what happened? He's like, the reason why I closed so well is I ask people to say yes thousands of times my presentation from the second I start my presentation, I'm asking them all these questions and he calls them trial closes to get them to say yes. He's like, if you watch when I'm speaking, everybody's heads in the room the entire time right. It's like a C of P heads bobbing the whole time. And do it by asking little yes questions. So up to this point so far, you guys, just from like a timing standpoint, everything you've done at this point should take about 15 minutes. I've done the title. I've built some rapport. I've done the ruler of the domino statement. I've qualified myself. I told my origin story. I likened to them. I've given them a case study. That's about 15 minutes to get that part done. So from a timing standpoint, now I'm in a transition too. So what I just did, this whole first thing is this is my first shot to push down the big domino and some people getting like they fell and they're sold and everything else. They're just getting more and more excited by the end. They're like, can you just give me the payment link? I can get those. You guys get those? It goes all the time. People are like, can you just give me the link to buy? Like I am so sold. I just want this. What's the link? And they're freaking out. So like we'll just send it through the chat to people and it's really, really fun. We'll close in deals before we get to the pitch. That's the initial goal. It's knock down the big domino. But then the rest of people are like, oh, this is awesome. Oh, wait, hold on. They start freaking out. Like, and also all these things start blocking. Right? The vehicle, false beliefs, the internal and the external. That's when we transition out to the content teaching section of the presentation. Now the biggest thing, and I think most of you guys understand this, but new people first learned, and it's the hardest thing they always do is that they want to shift into teacher mode. Okay. And they're like, oh, I'm going to go teach people all these cool things. And it's like understanding that the way you are teaching people is not through like, let me teach you step one, you're going to do this step two. Like that's what all of us as educators want to do. We want to get into the teaching mode. That is the fastest way to destroy yourselves. Okay. So you're teaching people by saying you have a false belief pattern. You have this thing that's wrong. This is the story you're telling yourself. This is the new story I'm going to tell you to try to replace that. And what's interesting is that oftentimes you as the teacher, the person leading this, by the time it's done, you're like, I didn't feel like I taught these guys anything, right? Because you didn't teach them like any step by step. What you did is you completely transformed their belief patterns, which for somebody who's experiencing that is worth a thousand times more than any of like step one, you're going to do this and step two, like you transition. Everything that they've held, they believed at this point in their life, they realized it's not actually correct. And this is the truth. That is like where, that's where all these huge things happen. That's where people have the big aha moments and they actually transform their lives. Okay. And then in the content, you do the teaching, all that kind of stuff. But if you bring all the teaching here, which is like a lot of you guys asking your default, is switching to teacher mode. In fact, most webinars that you guys have done that have failed, circles of people brought to me, all I do really quick as I scrubbed forward to the teaching section, I watched them within like five seconds. I'm like, you're teaching. That's not how you get people to change. Okay. When they give you money, then you can teach them. We're trying to break their false belief patterns and make them, and like give them this, this shift. That's how we're teaching them at this point. Okay. So first it's going to be weird for some of you guys, but as soon as you do that and you get the feedback, I always get people like, when I teach a good thing and I have tons of stuff, they'll be like, Oh, that was awesome. I got a lot out of it and then they don't buy. When I give them this transformational shift, like dude, that changed my life. I'm like, really? I didn't teach you anything. They're like, no, you, you like, I thought this is the way that the world worked. And now I understand it works like this. That opens up all these possibilities. Because that's what we're trying to do through this is open their mind to the possibilities and you're not going to feel like it's teaching, but they will because it's going to be such a big transformation for them. All right. So, and then I move right on. So that's kind of how I do, how I do that one. So let's take two minutes and plug in your three secrets that you guys created earlier today. One thing that took our webinar from terrible to converting and doing really, really well is I was teaching too much. And then I realized like there still should be curiosity to the how. So with your secrets, you're telling them really why they should do it, not the how they should do it, because the how is your product. So like you should eat carbs because here's why you should eat carbs, but I'm not telling them how to do it. Like I'm not telling them how to work into their day or what their plan should look like. I'm telling them why they should lift weights, but I'm not giving them a plan to lift weights. I'm just like, they're saying, okay, I need to lift weights, but how do I do it? Right? And they still don't know. So that's actually where your product falls. And when I started thinking of that, I started teaching less and just like leaving them on a cliffhanger or more. So like now I have to lift weights, but I don't know how to lift weights, right? And then that's when your product comes in. Yeah, exactly. So that's what really helped me just teach as less as possible. Awesome. Thank you. I love that. So you're teaching the why, these stories are just explaining the why so the product can teach them the how. Okay, let's transition now. So now we've stayed the three secrets. And this is where we start taking like, Hey, I want to, like now that you view the world, it's kind of like a matrix is like red pill, green pill. It's like, you just took the green pill, right? And it's like, wow, but now what? Okay. And like the worst thing you could possibly do is not sell somebody at that point. Okay. Something like everyone's got this weird thing with it. Like, I don't want to sell anything. Like, like if you care about your people, like you need to sell them something because they're going to see the world in a whole different way and have no tools or actually ability to implement it. And if you don't give them something at that point, like you just kind of screwed them over. And sometimes there's some, I don't think in this group, we have too much of that, but some people have a lot of like resistance to the selling. But that's in my mind, selling is like the highest level of service. Like that's where I'm getting you. And I'm going to, I'm going to force you to make a financial commitment so you can actually live and get that result we talked about. Cause I've painted the picture, like you're in, you want that. You believe this is a vehicle, but if I don't get you to commit with your wallet, you're never going to get there. Okay. In fact, the section of the book I call your moral obligation, like this is your, if you honestly believe that the vehicle that you are putting people into is the best thing for their success. The J-Bram said, like you have a moral obligation to do everything within your power to get people, to give you money so they can get in that vehicle, so they can get their result. Not about you. I believe my message, a hundred percent. That's why I'm so consistent with it. Like I believe that click funnels, that funnel, like this whole thing that we do is, is the vehicle that people need to transform the world. So that's why I have no shame in selling this thing like crazy. Cause I know the effect it's had on people. And so you need to have that same kind of commitment and belief in what you're doing. And if you do, then this car becomes fun and easy. If you don't, it becomes all nervous and like, oh, you'll freak out. But for me, like this is my favorite part of it. So when I transition, I'm coming through here and there's always this like weird awkwardness when you transition from like you doing the teaching to the selling part. Uh, so in Brandon yesterday, like I, you know, like going to my wife's, uh, uh, home parties, she's invited all the home parties and I always ask if I can tag along. And I want to sit in the back cause I love watching like the people do the home parties and they do this, good. They do this good. And they get outside and all of a sudden they start transitioning to the, to like, where they're going to ask people for money and like, they get nervous and they start sweating and like, they get like all fidgety. And it's like the funniest thing in the world. Cause none of them know how to make the transition point. But if you do it right, like the transition is really, really easy. And I don't want you guys to ever have that excuse again. And then we bomb. And then no one's asked that question anymore, which is nice for me. And how many years know what like, what your number one question is after you share your masterclass? Is there one thing that like, you know, people are going to bug you with every single time? Like, anyway, I'm not going to make you figure out that thing is, but there's something that's always you're going to get every time you do it. Okay. Is this helpful to keep moving through these? You want me to stop and I go, what's better for you guys to think? Just keep moving. Okay. All right. So now I've shared the first thing. So now I'm going to go as I'm going to go and I'm going to show my stack slide. Number one, the psychology behind the stack slide, I learned this initially from Armand Moran is maybe more money, collectively, probably than anything else I've ever done. The first time you do it, you will feel weird and awkward and embarrassed, but you will get over it when you're cashing checks that are bigger than anything you're dreamt of. So psychology behind it, when most people speak, that haven't learned this process through either me or through Armand, I think only guys have ever taught it, but what most people do is they talk about their offer and they talk about the bonuses and they go through all this stuff. And then the last thing they show, they show bonus like number five or six or 10 or whatever it is. And then they talk about the price. And the problem is our human minds are very, we can remember things not like, it's very short of our memory. So what happens is like, here's the best thing I got, then bonus number two, number three, number four, and this is my smallest bonus. And then this is the price. And what happens is that your brain associates the price with the last thing it saw. And so if the last thing you offer them isn't worth that much, then they're not going to buy. And so Armand taught me, I was out speaking, doing some stuff. And, and he was speaking on a stage, it was a Dan Kennedy stage. Before he got up, he's like, Hey man, I want to show you something. I'm like, what's that? He's like, it's called the stack. I'm like, what is it? He's like, just watch. He got up on stage and watching Armand, who's like one of the first guys I learned tons of this speaking from, he goes through, does this whole pitch and I'm watching it and he gets to the stack and it was like, I didn't know what it was, but it's this weird thing. And it was like, super fag. All this was happening. I didn't know what it was. Then he closed. I saw this room of 1200 people. I saw more than half of them jump up and run to the back and sign up for his thing. And I'm sitting there back just watching, like doing the math, counting everything. And it was done. I waited every kind of left and he walked over and he had this big old stack, fast stack of order forms this big. And he walked over and he's like, do you see that? I was like, what was that? He's like, it was the stack. He's like, do you want order forms like this? I'm like, yeah, he's like, did you got to learn the stack? And he kind of walked away. I'm like, dude, what's the stack? He's like, he's like, all right, I'll tell you. And he told me what I told you guys about the whole memory thing. He's like, watch what I do. He's like, I have a slide where I talk about my number one thing and I talk about it and then I stack it. Right. So I, so I'll show you the stack slide, which is kind of jacked up because of the keynote thing. So I apologize. It looks ugly, but conceptually, here's the first thing. Then you introduce the second thing you talk about it and you come back to the slide. Now you stack the second thing and the third thing, the fourth, and by the time it's done, I have all these things. I stack them all together and that's my introduced price. Now they're just like, they're rationalizing the price with the entire thing, not just the last thing you talked about. Okay. And so you're going to feel kind of weird. I promise you will feel, do you guys feel weird the first time you did it? It's kind of weird. Yeah. I didn't like it either. In fact, the Armand told me I was speaking to his event two weeks later. He said, he said, if you speak at my stage, you have to use the stack because I need you to make money. And I was like, all right. So he taught it to me, sent me his slides. I like learned it and went to that next event. I did the stack and I was the number one seller at his event. I made more money on stage in one time than I ever made prior. And it was cool. It was, I don't know if Brent's here, but Brent came to that event with me and he was sitting there and we got done and everything happened. Like I had my big stack of order forms. I think I made, it was like $160,000 in sales. And I got half that's like 80 grand was mine to take home. He was like, dude, you made 80 grand in 90 minutes. That's insane. I was like, I know dude, it was the stack. Like this is magic. And ever since then I've never not done, actually it's not true. One time I like, I'd done the stack a bunch of times it worked. And one time I was like, kind of embarrassed. I'm like, ah, it's not going to work. I'm just going to, and I didn't do it. And I blanked on stage. It was the most embarrassing thing in my life. And I swore that day I will never, ever give a presentation. I don't do the stacking in. So I get literally everything I've ever done. Like I can contribute more money to this one concept than anything else I've done. So like just use it before we're the first time and by the second or third time, you'll love it and then you'll never want to leave it away. Cause it's just, it's so worth it. Ted, so talk about my masterclass and I stack it. So that means you're going to get the six week funnel-hacks masterclass total of value of $2,997. Okay. And then I introduced now the tool. So for me, again, when I do this in real life, I flip these two, but for most situations works. The next thing, secondly, I'm going to give you guys, how many of you guys want to use ClickFunnels here? How many of you guys will ever use ClickFunnels for free? Cool. Um, one of my other favorite ones, people always like, well, can I finance this? I'm like, I don't finance things, but I do know someone who will finance it for you. Uh, it's called Visa Mastercard. You ever heard of them? They will finance this whole entire thing for you. And the coolest thing about Visa Mastercard, um, in fact, the way I tell, I was like, when I first got started online, I wanted to buy a Mark Jordan as my, was my mentor and he had a thousand dollar course I wanted to buy it. I didn't have it. I emailed him asking me to finance it. And his assistant rolled back and saying, we don't finance things that Visa Mastercard do. I was like, well, how does that work? Like that's kind of weird. He said, well, what's the minimum monthly payment on a thousand dollar product? I'm like, I don't know what 50 bucks. Okay. So, so you put it on a credit card, Visa Mastercard funds it in 30 days. Now you have to pay $50. Like, are you okay in 30 days now? If you're going through the training, maybe you haven't made money yet. I've been 30 days. Can you afford $50? I'm like, yeah, I can afford 50 bucks. Okay. Cool. Keep going through it. And then it's 60 days. Do you think you'll make 50 bucks? Yeah, I think so. Okay. So if you made 50 bucks, you pay that from the profits you made. And that point forward, your, your business will be paying for those payments. So it's now free. You paid $50 for this course. Okay. Now, if you didn't make it after 60 days, what do you think? Can you pay $50 and 60 days from now? Yeah, I think so. Okay. That gives you 30 more days. The next 30 days, you think that by the, now you're 90 days into 90 days, you think you could have, you'll be able to make at least 50 bucks from your business?
